questions.text: Consider Microsoft in the 1990s, which dominated the software industry by selling packaged products, and eBay, which grew rapidly by connecting buyers and sellers through its online marketplace. How did network effects give eBay a strategic advantage over Microsoft's traditional pipeline model?

answers.text:
743

    
         
      
        eBay’s network effects increased marginal costs as more users participated, while Microsoft enjoyed stable software production costs.
        
      
    
    
      
    
    
      eBay’s network effects increased marginal costs as more users participated, while Microsoft enjoyed stable software production costs.
      
       
      
              
      
      
    
    
      exact_answer
      none
      7404796
      743
      
    
      
        0 (with margin: 0)
      
      
        
          0
         (with precision: 10)
      
      
        Between 0 and 0
      
    
      
      
        margin of error
        
          +/-
        
        
      
    
      0

844

    
         
      
        eBay’s network effects allowed it to grow exponentially as more users joined, while Microsoft’s growth was constrained by linear increases in software production and distribution.
        
      
    
    
      
    
    
      eBay’s network effects allowed it to grow exponentially as more users joined, while Microsoft’s growth was constrained by linear increases in software production and distribution.
